fragility_points: A sudden macro or crypto shock could erase the cushion before noon ET.
Assignment snapshot price and fetched page price were not identical.
key_assumptions: No sharp downside move pushes Binance BTC/USDT below 70000 at the exact noon-ET resolving minute.
Current Binance spot around 72.3k is a reasonable anchor for the remaining horizon.
Binance-specific price behavior will not diverge enough from broader BTC trading to change the resolving candle outcome.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket rules make Binance BTC/USDT 12:00 ET 1-minute close the governing source of truth.
Binance direct data showed BTC/USDT around 72.3k, leaving a meaningful cushion over 70000.
With less than a day left, that distance plus recent price regime supports a high Yes probability.
Residual risk is concentrated in exact-minute timing and short-horizon downside volatility rather than base directional weakness.
main_thesis: BTC/USDT was already comfortably above 70000 on Binance with less than a day left, so a high-Yes view is justified, though exact-minute timestamp risk keeps confidence below the most aggressive market pricing.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to 'Yes' if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone has a final 'Close' price higher than the price specified in the title.
Binance BTC/USDT spot was about 72,275 at review time.
source_quality_view: Good for a medium-difficulty case: one primary rules source plus direct Binance market data, with moderate evidence independence and low-medium source-of-truth ambiguity.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact 1-minute Binance candle close at noon ET, so a brief sharp selloff could still flip the result.
Very high market confidence can be a bit overextended for a narrow timestamp market.
strongest_supports: Binance BTC/USDT spot was about 72275 at review time, roughly 3.2% above the threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes were mostly already above 70000.
A simple realized-volatility check made 70000 look materially below spot over the remaining horizon.
timing_relevance: High: this is a date-sensitive, exact-minute noon-ET candle-close market, so timing mechanics materially affect risk.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether the true live market-implied baseline at assignment time was closer to 84.5% or the fetched page's ~93% display.
Whether Binance should have a clean canonical entity slug in-vault.
what_would_change_view: I would lower the estimate if BTC/USDT moved down toward 70000, if downside volatility rose materially into the final hours, or if Binance-specific resolution mechanics looked more ambiguous than the rules imply.
